f = 13 – cm 0625/51 October/November 2020 MP1 A t least 3 different metals (or alloys) suggested MP2 Correct circuit diagram to include test wire, power source, ammeter and voltmete r MP3 Correct symbols used for ammeter and voltmeter. MP4 Determine resistance of a wire by measuring potential difference and current MP5 Repeat with at least two more wires of different material. MP6 Key variables: Any one from: Length of wire Diameter/thickness of wire MP7 Table with columns for material, potential difference, current and resistance with correct units Additional graph notes : NOTE: The principle to apply here is ‘could I draw a significant ly better line, using these points, under examination condition s?’ If the answer is definitely ‘yes’, do not award the mark. NOTE: If candidate’s scale consists of actual readings at equal intervals this will produce a perfect straight line! The only m ark available in this case is the first (axes right way round and labelled) So maximum 1. – If axes are wrong way round, the other 3 marks are still available.
